en_definition: a small portable computer
example1: I do all my homework on my laptop.
example1_ru: Я делаю всю домашнюю работу на ноутбуке.
example2: Her laptop stopped working right before the exam.

en_definition: a small flat computer with a touchscreen
example1: He reads textbooks on his tablet during commutes.
example1_ru: Он читает учебники на планшете во время поездок.
example2: The tablet is lighter than carrying paper books.

en_definition: a combination of keys that performs a task quickly
example1: Learning keyboard shortcuts saves a lot of time.
example1_ru: Изучение сочетаний клавиш экономит много времени.
example2: She used a keyboard shortcut to copy the text.

en_definition: a screen that responds when you touch it with your finger
example1: The touchscreen on my phone cracked last week.
example1_ru: Сенсорный экран моего телефона треснул на прошлой неделе.
example2: He tapped the touchscreen to open the map app.

en_definition: the amount of digital space available for files on a device
example1: I need more storage space for my photos.
example1_ru: Мне нужно больше места для хранения моих фотографий.
example2: The phone warned that storage space was almost full.

en_definition: online space used to save files instead of on a device
example1: We keep our group project on cloud storage.
example1_ru: Мы храним наш групповой проект в облачном хранилище.
example2: Cloud storage lets you access files from any device.

en_definition: a saved copy of data used in case the original is lost
example1: Always keep a backup file of your thesis.
example1_ru: Всегда храни резервную копию своей дипломной работы.
example2: I lost my essay because I forgot to make a backup file.

en_definition: an agreement with a phone company for a fixed amount of internet use
example1: My data plan includes ten gigabytes per month.
example1_ru: Мой тарифный план включает десять гигабайт в месяц.
example2: We compared different data plans before choosing one.

en_definition: a device that connects computers and phones to the internet
example1: The router in our dorm needs to be restarted.
example1_ru: Роутер в нашем общежитии нужно перезагрузить.
example2: We share one router between four rooms.

en_definition: a network that connects devices without cables, using radio signals
example1: Connect to the wireless network using the campus password.
example1_ru: Подключись к беспроводной сети, используя пароль кампуса.
example2: The wireless network is often slow in the evening.

en_definition: a wave or message sent, especially through a network
example1: There's no phone signal in the basement.
example1_ru: В подвале нет сигнала телефона.
example2: The signal gets weaker at the back of the building.

en_definition: a section of a browser window showing a separate web page
example1: I usually have too many browser tabs open.
example1_ru: У меня обычно открыто слишком много вкладок браузера.
example2: Close a few browser tabs to speed up the laptop.

en_definition: a website used to find information on the internet
example1: She used a search engine to find the article.
example1_ru: Она использовала поисковую систему, чтобы найти статью.
example2: The search engine showed thousands of results.

en_definition: a single document available on the internet
example1: The web page didn't load because of poor internet.
example1_ru: Веб-страница не загрузилась из-за плохого интернета.
example2: Bookmark this web page for later reading.

en_definition: the unique string used to send and receive email
example1: Please write your email address clearly on the form.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, напишите свой адрес электронной почты чётко в форме.
example2: I typed my email address wrong the first time.

en_definition: the place in an email account where new messages appear
example1: Check your inbox for the confirmation email.
example1_ru: Проверь входящие на наличие письма с подтверждением.
example2: My inbox was full of unread messages.

en_definition: a document sent together with an email
example1: The attachment file was too large to send.
example1_ru: Прикреплённый файл был слишком большим, чтобы отправить.
example2: Please open the attachment file before the meeting.

en_definition: an email that has been started but not sent yet
example1: I saved the message as a draft email.
example1_ru: Я сохранил сообщение как черновик письма.
example2: Her draft email sat unfinished for two days.

en_definition: the place where unwanted or junk emails are automatically sent
example1: Check your spam folder if you can't find the message.
example1_ru: Проверь папку со спамом, если не можешь найти сообщение.
example2: The invitation ended up in my spam folder.

en_definition: a check performed by software to find harmful programs
example1: Run a virus scan before opening unknown files.
example1_ru: Запусти проверку на вирусы перед открытием незнакомых файлов.
example2: The virus scan found nothing dangerous on my laptop.

en_definition: a shop that sells tools and equipment for the home
example1: We bought a new lamp at the hardware store.
example1_ru: Мы купили новую лампу в хозяйственном магазине.
example2: The hardware store near campus sells cheap tools.

en_definition: a small useful device, especially electronic
example1: He loves buying new gadgets for his room.
example1_ru: Он обожает покупать новые гаджеты для своей комнаты.
example2: This little gadget charges three devices at once.

en_definition: a long flexible tool used to measure length
example1: We used a measuring tape to check the desk's size.
example1_ru: Мы использовали измерительную ленту, чтобы проверить размер стола.
example2: The measuring tape rolled back into its case.

en_definition: to find out how heavy something is
example1: The clerk weighed the package before mailing it.
example1_ru: Клерк взвесил посылку перед отправкой.
example2: This bag weighs almost ten kilograms.

en_definition: how long something is from one end to the other
example1: The length of the room is about five meters.
example1_ru: Длина комнаты около пяти метров.
example2: Measure the length of the desk before buying a cover.

en_definition: how wide something is from side to side
example1: The width of the door is not enough for the sofa.
example1_ru: Ширина двери недостаточна для дивана.
example2: Check the width of the shelf before you order it.

en_definition: how tall a person or thing is
example1: The height of the building is around fifty meters.
example1_ru: Высота здания около пятидесяти метров.
example2: Her height makes her good at basketball.

en_definition: how deep something is from top to bottom
example1: The depth of the pool is two meters here.
example1_ru: Глубина бассейна здесь два метра.
example2: Scientists measured the depth of the lake.

en_definition: the amount of space between two places or things
example1: The distance between the dorm and campus is short.
example1_ru: Расстояние между общежитием и кампусом небольшое.
example2: We measured the distance to the nearest station.

en_definition: the fastest speed a vehicle is legally allowed to travel
example1: The speed limit near the school is very low.
example1_ru: Ограничение скорости возле школы очень низкое.
example2: He got a fine for breaking the speed limit.

en_definition: a smaller copy of something built to exact proportions
example1: The students built a scale model of the bridge.
example1_ru: Студенты построили масштабную модель моста.
example2: The scale model helped explain how the engine works.

en_definition: a substance that flows freely, like water
example1: Airlines limit the amount of liquid in carry-on bags.
example1_ru: Авиакомпании ограничивают количество жидкости в ручной клади.
example2: The blue liquid in the bottle smelled like cleaning fluid.

en_definition: having a firm shape, not liquid or gas
example1: Ice is water in its solid form.
example1_ru: Лёд — это вода в твёрдой форме.
example2: The table is made of solid wood.

en_definition: a substance, like air, that has no fixed shape or volume
example1: The stove uses natural gas for cooking.
example1_ru: Плита использует природный газ для готовки.
example2: Steam is water turned into gas.

en_definition: to change from a solid to a liquid because of heat
example1: The ice cream started to melt in the sun.
example1_ru: Мороженое начало таять на солнце.
example2: Snow melts quickly once spring arrives.

en_definition: to change from a liquid to a solid because of cold
example1: Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius.
example1_ru: Вода замерзает при нуле градусов Цельсия.
example2: The lake froze completely during the coldest week.

en_definition: to mix completely into a liquid and disappear
example1: Sugar will dissolve in hot tea quickly.
example1_ru: Сахар быстро растворится в горячем чае.
example2: Salt dissolves in water more easily than in oil.

en_definition: the temperature at which a liquid turns into gas
example1: The boiling point of water is one hundred degrees Celsius.
example1_ru: Точка кипения воды — сто градусов Цельсия.
example2: Altitude can change the boiling point of water.

en_definition: the force that pulls objects toward the ground
example1: Gravity keeps our feet on the ground.
example1_ru: Гравитация удерживает наши ноги на земле.
example2: The apple fell because of gravity.

en_definition: an object that attracts iron and steel
example1: The fridge door is covered with small magnets.
example1_ru: Дверь холодильника покрыта маленькими магнитами.
example2: A magnet picked up all the small nails.

en_definition: a form of energy used for light, heat, and machines
example1: The storm cut off electricity for two hours.
example1_ru: Буря отключила электричество на два часа.
example2: This device runs on electricity, not batteries.

en_definition: a device that stores and provides electrical power
example1: The remote control needs a new battery.
example1_ru: Пульту нужна новая батарейка.
example2: My phone battery dies quickly in cold weather.

en_definition: a small device used to turn something on or off
example1: The light switch is next to the door.
example1_ru: Выключатель света рядом с дверью.
example2: Flip the switch to turn on the fan.

en_definition: a path that allows electricity to flow
example1: The lamp didn't work because of a broken circuit.
example1_ru: Лампа не работала из-за разомкнутой цепи.
example2: We built a simple circuit in our science class.

en_definition: the part of a cable that connects to a power outlet
example1: The plug on my charger is slightly bent.
example1_ru: Вилка моего зарядного устройства немного погнута.
example2: Insert the plug carefully into the outlet.

en_definition: a device that connects equipment that would not normally fit together
example1: I need an adapter for my laptop charger abroad.
example1_ru: Мне нужен переходник для зарядки ноутбука за границей.
example2: The hotel lent us a plug adapter for the night.

en_definition: an instrument used to see very small objects clearly
example1: We looked at the cells under a microscope.
example1_ru: Мы рассматривали клетки под микроскопом.
example2: The lab has ten microscopes for student use.

en_definition: an instrument used to see distant objects like stars
example1: We used a telescope to look at the moon.
example1_ru: Мы использовали телескоп, чтобы рассмотреть луну.
example2: The astronomy club has its own telescope.

en_definition: a small glass tube used in chemistry experiments
example1: The chemist filled the test tube with a blue liquid.
example1_ru: Химик наполнил пробирку синей жидкостью.
example2: Be careful when heating a test tube over a flame.

en_definition: a glass container used in a laboratory to measure and mix liquids
example1: Pour the water carefully into the beaker.
example1_ru: Аккуратно налей воду в химический стакан.
example2: The beaker cracked after being heated too fast.

en_definition: protective glasses worn to keep eyes safe during experiments
example1: Always wear safety goggles in the chemistry lab.
example1_ru: Всегда надевай защитные очки в химической лаборатории.
example2: She put on safety goggles before mixing the chemicals.

en_definition: a long protective coat worn during scientific work
example1: Students must wear a lab coat during experiments.
example1_ru: Студенты должны носить лабораторный халат во время экспериментов.
example2: He left his lab coat on the hook by the door.

en_definition: a device used to measure a period of time
example1: Set the timer for ten minutes during the reaction.
example1_ru: Поставь таймер на десять минут во время реакции.
example2: The timer beeped when the experiment was finished.

en_definition: a diagram showing the relationship between numbers or data
example1: Draw a graph to show the change in temperature.
example1_ru: Нарисуй график, показывающий изменение температуры.
example2: The graph clearly showed a rising trend.

en_definition: one of the two lines used to show data on a graph
example1: Label the x-axis with the time in minutes.
example1_ru: Подпиши ось x временем в минутах.
example2: The vertical axis shows temperature in degrees.

en_definition: to mark a single value on a graph
example1: Plot a point for each measurement you record.
example1_ru: Нанеси точку для каждого измерения, которое ты записываешь.
example2: She plotted a point for every hour of the experiment.

en_definition: a chart that uses bars to compare different values
example1: The bar chart compared sales across four months.
example1_ru: Столбчатая диаграмма сравнивала продажи за четыре месяца.
example2: Each bar chart column represents a different country.

en_definition: a circular chart divided into slices to show proportions
example1: The pie chart shows how students spend their free time.
example1_ru: Круговая диаграмма показывает, как студенты проводят свободное время.
example2: Each slice of the pie chart represents a subject.

en_definition: a set of symbols showing a mathematical or scientific rule
example1: Remember the formula for finding the area of a circle.
example1_ru: Запомни формулу для нахождения площади круга.
example2: She wrote the chemical formula on the board.

en_definition: a mathematical statement showing that two expressions are equal
example1: Solve this equation for the value of x.
example1_ru: Реши это уравнение относительно x.
example2: The teacher explained the equation step by step.

en_definition: the number that, multiplied by itself, gives a given number
example1: The square root of nine is three.
example1_ru: Квадратный корень из девяти равен трём.
example2: We learned how to find a square root without a calculator.

en_definition: a part of a whole number, like one half
example1: Write the answer as a simple fraction.
example1_ru: Запиши ответ в виде простой дроби.
example2: Half is a common fraction used every day.

en_definition: the dot or comma used to separate whole numbers from parts
example1: Don't forget the decimal point in your answer.
example1_ru: Не забудь десятичную точку в своём ответе.
example2: The price was written with two digits after the decimal point.

en_definition: a small device or app used to do math quickly
example1: You can use a calculator during this part of the exam.
example1_ru: На этой части экзамена можно использовать калькулятор.
example2: She forgot her calculator on exam day.

en_definition: a chart that organizes all known chemical elements
example1: The periodic table is on the wall of the lab.
example1_ru: Периодическая таблица висит на стене лаборатории.
example2: Students must memorize part of the periodic table.

en_definition: a basic substance that cannot be broken into simpler parts
example1: Oxygen is an element we need to breathe.
example1_ru: Кислород — элемент, который нам нужен для дыхания.
example2: The teacher listed ten common elements on the board.

en_definition: a combination of two or more substances
example1: Salt water is a simple mixture.
example1_ru: Солёная вода — простая смесь.
example2: The mixture turned green after we added the chemical.

en_definition: the smallest unit of a living thing
example1: Every living thing is made up of cells.
example1_ru: Все живые существа состоят из клеток.
example2: We viewed plant cells under the microscope.

en_definition: any living thing, such as a plant, animal, or bacteria
example1: Bacteria are tiny living organisms found everywhere.
example1_ru: Бактерии — крошечные живые организмы, встречающиеся повсюду.
example2: Every organism needs energy to survive.

en_definition: a group of animals or plants that share the same features
example1: This species of bird only lives in warm climates.
example1_ru: Этот вид птиц живёт только в тёплом климате.
example2: Scientists discovered a new species of insect.

en_definition: the natural home of an animal or plant
example1: The forest is the natural habitat of many animals.
example1_ru: Лес — естественная среда обитания многих животных.
example2: Pollution can destroy an animal's habitat.

en_definition: all the living things in an area and how they interact
example1: A pond is a small but complete ecosystem.
example1_ru: Пруд — маленькая, но полноценная экосистема.
example2: Cutting down trees can damage a whole ecosystem.

en_definition: harmful substances added to air, water, or land
example1: Air pollution is a serious problem in big cities.
example1_ru: Загрязнение воздуха — серьёзная проблема в больших городах.
example2: The river suffers from heavy industrial pollution.

en_definition: energy from sources that never run out, like sun or wind
example1: Solar power is a common type of renewable energy.
example1_ru: Солнечная энергия — распространённый вид возобновляемой энергии.
example2: The country is investing more in renewable energy.

en_definition: a device that turns sunlight into electricity
example1: The roof is covered with solar panels.
example1_ru: Крыша покрыта солнечными панелями.
example2: Solar panels can lower your electricity bill.

en_definition: a container used to collect items for recycling
example1: Put the bottles in the blue recycling bin.
example1_ru: Положи бутылки в синий контейнер для переработки.
example2: The recycling bin near the dorm was overflowing.

en_definition: the total amount of harmful gases produced by a person's activities
example1: Taking the bus reduces your carbon footprint.
example1_ru: Поездка на автобусе снижает твой углеродный след.
example2: Our class learned how to lower our carbon footprint.

en_definition: long-term changes in the earth's weather patterns
example1: Climate change is affecting weather all over the world.
example1_ru: Изменение климата влияет на погоду по всему миру.
example2: The lecture explained the causes of climate change.

en_definition: the gradual rise in the earth's average temperature
example1: Global warming is causing ice to melt faster.
example1_ru: Глобальное потепление заставляет лёд таять быстрее.
example2: Scientists study the effects of global warming closely.

en_definition: a written document describing an experiment and its results
example1: Submit your laboratory report by Friday afternoon.
example1_ru: Сдай лабораторный отчёт до пятницы, до полудня.
example2: The laboratory report must include a clear conclusion.

en_definition: a collection of related information used for analysis
example1: We analyzed a large data set for our project.
example1_ru: Мы проанализировали большой набор данных для нашего проекта.
example2: The professor shared a data set for practice.

en_definition: an assignment done together by several students
example1: Our group project is about renewable energy sources.
example1_ru: Наш групповой проект посвящён источникам возобновляемой энергии.
example2: The group project taught us how to work as a team.

en_definition: the combined effort of people working together
example1: The project needed good teamwork to finish on time.
example1_ru: Проекту требовалась хорошая командная работа, чтобы завершиться вовремя.
example2: Teamwork helped us solve the problem faster.

en_definition: to think of many ideas quickly, often in a group
example1: Let's brainstorm ideas for the presentation topic.
example1_ru: Давай проведём мозговой штурм для темы презентации.
example2: We brainstormed for twenty minutes before choosing a topic.

en_definition: a single page used in a digital presentation
example1: Each presentation slide should have only a few words.
example1_ru: Каждый слайд презентации должен содержать всего несколько слов.
example2: She added a picture to the last presentation slide.

en_definition: the activity of giving a speech in front of an audience
example1: Public speaking still makes me a little nervous.
example1_ru: Публичное выступление всё ещё немного нервирует меня.
example2: He took a class to improve his public speaking.

en_definition: the way people communicate through movement and posture
example1: Good body language makes a presentation more convincing.
example1_ru: Хороший язык тела делает презентацию более убедительной.
example2: Her nervous body language showed she was worried.

en_definition: looking directly at someone's eyes while speaking
example1: Make eye contact with the audience during your talk.
example1_ru: Поддерживай зрительный контакт с аудиторией во время выступления.
example2: He avoided eye contact because he felt shy.

en_definition: a meeting where people give opinions about someone's work
example1: The teacher held a feedback session after the presentation.
example1_ru: Учитель провёл сессию обратной связи после презентации.
example2: I found the feedback session really useful for my essay.

en_definition: helpful comments that point out how to improve
example1: Her constructive criticism helped me fix the report.
example1_ru: Её конструктивная критика помогла мне исправить отчёт.
example2: Good teachers give constructive criticism, not just praise.

en_definition: a person of the same age or status as you
example1: We shared our essays with a peer for feedback.
example1_ru: Мы поделились своими эссе с ровесником для обратной связи.
example2: Working with a peer made the task easier.

en_definition: an experienced person who guides and advises someone less experienced
example1: My mentor helped me choose the right courses.
example1_ru: Мой наставник помог мне выбрать правильные курсы.
example2: She became a mentor for first-year students.

en_definition: the time when a teacher is available to answer questions
example1: Visit the professor during office hours if you're confused.
example1_ru: Посети преподавателя в приёмные часы, если ты запутался.
example2: Office hours are usually on Tuesday afternoons.

en_definition: additional points given for optional extra work
example1: You can earn extra credit by writing a summary.
example1_ru: Ты можешь заработать дополнительные баллы, написав резюме.
example2: She did the extra credit assignment to raise her grade.

en_definition: the overall mark given at the end of a course
example1: Your final grade depends on both exams and homework.
example1_ru: Твоя итоговая оценка зависит и от экзаменов, и от домашних заданий.
example2: He was happy with his final grade in physics.

en_definition: the official history of a student's grades and courses
example1: Your academic record affects your scholarship chances.
example1_ru: Твоя академическая успеваемость влияет на шансы получить стипендию.
example2: Keep a copy of your academic record for applications.

en_definition: a preparatory year of study before a full degree program
example1: International students often complete a foundation year first.
example1_ru: Иностранные студенты часто сначала проходят подготовительный год.
example2: The foundation year helped improve my English and math skills.

en_definition: a student studying in a country different from their own
example1: International students meet weekly for language practice.
example1_ru: Иностранные студенты еженедельно встречаются для языковой практики.
example2: Our university welcomes many international students every year.

en_definition: a full course of study that leads to a diploma or degree
example1: This degree program takes four years to complete.
example1_ru: Эта программа обучения занимает четыре года.
example2: She chose a degree program in computer science.

en_definition: to study a particular subject as your main area at university
example1: He plans to major in chemistry next year.
example1_ru: В следующем году он планирует специализироваться на химии.
example2: She is majoring in international business.
